response.justification: Wei Chen's background is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js-focused, with no evidence of Kotlin experience and only surface-level Java exposure (robotic arm work at Altom, C/C++ IDE components at NI). There is no mention of the Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-depth work. The IDE experience at National Instruments involved a C-based toolchain (LabWindows/CVI), which does not translate to JVM language tooling or IntelliJ-platform-style development. The core technical requirements of this role—deep JVM expertise, Spring ecosystem, enterprise framework tooling—are essentially unmet.
strengths: Demonstrated IDE tooling exposure via National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI component development)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source testing libraries
Broad multi-language background shows adaptability across complex technical domains
concerns: No Kotlin experience; Java usage is minimal, brief, and not enterprise-context focused
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework work
Core expertise is TypeScript/Node.js — a fundamentally different ecosystem from what the role demands
